question: Define a C++ class named `Book` with private member variables for `title` (string), `author` (string), and `isbn` (string). Implement public methods to `setTitle`, `setAuthor`, `setISBN` and `displayBookInfo` which prints all details.

test_cases.expected:
Title: The Hitchhiker's Guide to the Galaxy
Author: Douglas Adams
ISBN: 978-0345391803

Title: 1984
Author: George Orwell
ISBN: 978-0451524935

errors: Setter methods pass string parameters by value, leading to unnecessary copying, which is inefficient for std::string.
Frequent use of std::endl in `displayBookInfo` causes unnecessary buffer flushes, impacting efficiency.
Parameter names in setters (t, a, i) are not descriptive, affecting readability.
The `displayBookInfo` method is not marked `const` despite not modifying the object's state, which is a minor quality oversight.
